title: Personnel of the Iranian Oil Industry by Company (Consortium/NIOC/Others) and Category of Work (Labor/Iranian staff/Non-Iranian staff), 1955-1961
source_org: US Bureau of Mines Information Circular 8203 (1963), Table 5 (p.21)
extraction_method: pdftoppm -r 150 render to PNG (PDF page 29) + Read tool visual verification. Every company-year subtotal cross-checked by arithmetic against Table 4's grand totals (usbm1963-oil-industry-employment-1939-1960) -- 5 of 6 years matched exactly; 1958 had a 5-unit source-internal discrepancy (62,033 printed vs. 62,028 from summing the three reported components), PRESERVED AS-IS rather than silently corrected, consistent with this project's no-fabrication/no-silent-reconciliation rule (same rule that previously preserved a 'households surveyed' inconsistency in Round 37's wb1974 extraction).
notes: Finer-grained companion to usbm1963-oil-industry-employment-1939-1960 -- splits the workforce by EMPLOYER (Consortium vs. NIOC vs. Others [Consortium contractors + SIRIP/IPAC/IRCAN]) and by CATEGORY (Labor vs. Iranian staff vs. Non-Iranian staff), not just by nationality alone. The source table itself uses inconsistent bracket-groupings across years (some years split Iranian/Non-Iranian staff, others report them combined, others combine Labor+Iranian-staff instead) -- every such grouping is preserved exactly as printed in the 'category' column label (e.g. 'Iranian staff + Non-Iranian staff (combined in source)') rather than guessing a split, per the no-fabrication rule. Combined with usbm1963-consortium-disbursements-1954-1962's 'Wages and salaries' row (aggregate GBP paid per year), a future data/processed/ harmonization pass could compute an implied average annual wage per Consortium worker (wage bill / Consortium total employment) -- deliberately not computed here, flagged for a future explicit derived-statistic step.
